item.title: 让我们学习 Django #4 使用模板和静态文件 | Let's Learn Django #4 Using templates & static files
item.description:
拉吉库拉纳

发表于 6 月 17 日

我们已经在之前的博客中看到了如何编写我们的第一个视图。 在此博客中，我们将探讨如何使用和呈现模板。

什么是模板？

模板只是 HTML 文件，我们可以将其呈现为对 URL/视图的响应。 我们将在下一篇博客中详细探讨

设置模板文件夹

设置模板文件夹非常简单。 首先，我们将在基本目录中创建一个名为templates的新文件夹。

接下来，我们需要告诉 Django 我们将使用这个文件夹来放置我们所有的模板。

为此，我们都导航到我们的 settings.py 文件。 有一个名为TEMPLATES的列表，它将有一个 JSON 对象，其中包含一个名为DIRS的字段，其中包含一个空列表。 我们只需要向其中添加templates （我们文件夹的名称）。 最后，它应该看起来像这样：

 TEMPLATES = [ { 'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates', 'DIRS': ['templates'], 'APP_DIRS': True, 'OPTIONS': { 'context_processors': [ 'django.template.context_processors.debug', 'django.template.context_processors.request', 'django.contrib.auth.context_processors.auth', 'django.contrib.messages.context_processors.messages', ], }, }, ]

这就是我们设置模板所需要做的。

现在我们将看到如何使用模板。

渲染模板

呈现模板意味着生成一个 HTML 响应，其中包含视图中定义的动态数据和逻辑，然后将其发送到用户的浏览器。

在上一篇博客中，我们看到了如何编写我们的观点。 所以现在，让我们渲染一个 HTML 模板作为对该视图的响应。

首先，我们将在模板目录中创建一个名为hello.html的新 HTML 文件

然后您可以添加任何您想要的 HTML 页面设计。 我会选择一个简单的。
 <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <title>Hello World!</title> </head> <body> <h1>Hello World!</h1> <p>My name is <strong>Rachit Khurana</strong></p> </body> </html>

现在，当用户转到索引页面时，我们将返回此模板。

为此，我们将导航到views.py ，我们在其中定义了一个 hello 函数，该函数在用户转到索引页面时被调用。

然后我们将使用django.shortcuts中的render函数来渲染这个模板。

它采用请求、模板文件路径和可选上下文作为参数，并返回带有呈现模板的 HTTP 响应。

现在，如果我们运行服务器并转到127.0.0.1:8000/ ，我们将能够将其视为 HTML 呈现的页面。 很棒吧？

但是，请注意，您将无法在模板文件夹中创建 CSS 或 JS 文件并在 HTML 文件中使用它们。 这是因为 Django 只获取 HTML 文件，并直接返回呈现的文件。 它不使用 CSS 和 JS 文件。 要使用单独的 CSS 和 JS 文件，我们需要将它们作为静态文件提供。

我们现在将看到如何提供静态文件。

静态文件

静态文件包括 CSS、JavaScript、图像和其他对所有访问您网站的用户保持不变的文件。 这些文件直接提供给客户的浏览器，并有助于网页的视觉和交互方面。

要设置静态文件，我们将创建一个名为static的新文件夹。

现在我们需要告诉 Django 使用这个文件夹来提供静态文件。 为此，我们将以下行添加到settings.py文件中：


 STATICFILES_DIRS = [ BASE_DIR / "static" ]

就是这样。 现在我们存储在静态文件夹中的任何文件都可以在http://127.0.0.1:8000/static/ <filename> 上找到。

在模板中使用静态文件

现在，让我们创建一个名为style.css的简单 CSS 文件。 我将使用一个非常简单的 CSS 文件进行演示。

 body{ background-color: #ca1c1c; color: aqua; }

现在，我们将通过使用静态标签在我们的hello.html文件中使用它。 首先，我们将导入它然后使用它，如下所示：

 {% load static %} <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <title>Hello World!</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="{% static 'style.css' %}"> </head> <body> <h1>Hello World!</h1> <p>My name is <strong>Rachit Khurana</strong></p> </body> </html>

现在，当我们运行服务器并转到127.0.0.1:8000/时，我们将看到带有 CSS 的新 HTML 页面。

恭喜，您知道如何呈现 HTML 模板和使用静态文件。 但是，如果我们需要将参数从代码传递给模板怎么办？

我们也可以轻松做到这一点。 让我们来看看如何做到这一点。

将参数传递给模板

我们可以轻松地将变量传递给模板进行渲染。

例如，我想将我的姓名和年龄传递给模板。 我们将转到views.py文件并在函数中声明这些变量。

然后我们需要将另一个参数传递给 render 函数，其中包含我们需要作为字典传递给模板的所有变量。 最终代码将如下所示：
 from django.shortcuts import render, HttpResponse # Create your views here. def hello(request): name="Rachit Khurana" age=19 return render(request, 'hello.html', {'name':name, 'age':age})

现在我们可以在模板hello.html中使用这些变量，方法是将它们用双大括号括起来，如下所示：

 {% load static %} <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <title>Hello World!</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="{% static 'style.css' %}"> </head> <body> <h1>Hello World!</h1> <p>My name is <strong>{{name}}</strong></p> <p>My age is <strong>{{age}}</strong></p> </body> </html>

这里{{name}}将被我们传递给模板的name值替换。 现在，如果我们尝试运行服务器并转到127.0.0.1:8000/ ，我们可以看到带有这些变量的新呈现的 HTML 页面。

像这样：

我们不仅可以传递字符串或整数值，还可以传递列表、元组、字典等。

恭喜，您现在知道如何呈现模板、提供静态文件以及将变量传递给模板。 但这还不是全部，Django 模板比这更强大。 我们将在下一篇博客中探讨更多相关信息。

一旦暂停，dilutewater 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，dilutewater 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，dilutewater 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dilutewater 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记稀释水的方法：

降低 dilutewater 的所有帖子的可见性

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging dilutewater will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflaging dilutewater 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Rachit Khurana

Posted on Jun 17

 

We have already seen how to write our first view in the previous blogs. In this blog, we will explore how to use & render templates.

What are templates?

Templates are simply HTML files that we can render as a response to a URL/view. Django templates offer a robust templating language with various built-in tags and filters that you can utilize to manipulate and display data in your HTML templates.

We will explore it in detail in the next blog

Setting up templates folder

Setting up a templates folder is quite easy. First, we will create a new folder in the base directory with the name templates.

Next, we need to tell Django that we will be using this folder for placing all our templates.

For that, we all navigate to our settings.py file. There is a list named TEMPLATES & it will have a JSON object with a field named DIRS that contain an empty list. We just need to add templates (the name of our folder) to it. Finally, it should look something like this:

TEMPLATES = [ { 'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates', 'DIRS': ['templates'], 'APP_DIRS': True, 'OPTIONS': { 'context_processors': [ 'django.template.context_processors.debug', 'django.template.context_processors.request', 'django.contrib.auth.context_processors.auth', 'django.contrib.messages.context_processors.messages', ], }, },
]

That is all we need to do to set up templates.

Now we will see how to use templates.

Rendering templates

Rendering a template means generating an HTML response that incorporates the dynamic data and logic defined in your views, which is then sent to the user's browser.

In the last blog, we saw how to write our views. So now, let's render an HTML template as a response to that view.

First, we will create a new HTML file in the templates directory with the name hello.html

You can then add any HTML page design you want. I will go with a simple one. 
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html> <head> <title>Hello World!</title> </head> <body> <h1>Hello World!</h1> <p>My name is <strong>Rachit Khurana</strong></p> </body>
</html>

Now, we will return this template when the user goes to the index page.

For that, we will navigate to views.py where we defined a hello function that gets called when the user goes to the index page.

We will then use render function from django.shortcuts to render this template.

The render function simplifies the process of rendering templates by handling the template loading and rendering process automatically. It takes the request, the template file path, and an optional context as arguments and returns an HTTP response with the rendered template.

The views.py code will look like this

from django.shortcuts import render, HttpResponse # Create your views here.
def hello(request): return render(request, 'hello.html')

Now if we run the server & go to 127.0.0.1:8000/, we will be able to see this as an HTML-rendered page. Awesome right?

However, do note that you won't be able to create CSS or JS files in the templates folder & use those in the HTML file. This is because Django only takes the HTML file, & returns the rendered file directly. It doesn't take the CSS & JS files. To use separate CSS & JS files, we need to serve them as static files.

We will now see how to serve static files.

Static Files

Static files include CSS, JavaScript, images, and other files that remain constant for all users visiting your website. These files are served directly to the client's browser and contribute to the visual and interactive aspects of your web pages.

To set up static files, we will create a new folder named static.

Now we need to tell Django to use this folder for serving static files. For that, we will add the following line to the settings.py file:


STATICFILES_DIRS = [ BASE_DIR / "static"
]

STATICFILES_DIRS is a list where you specify the directory paths where Django should look for static files.

That's it. Now whatever file we store in the static folder will be available at http://127.0.0.1:8000/static/<filename>.

Using static files inside templates

Now, let's create a simple CSS file with the name style.css. I will be using a pretty simple CSS file for demonstration.

body{ background-color: #ca1c1c; color: aqua;
}

Now, we will use this in our hello.html file by making use of the static tag. First, we will import it & then use it as shown below:

{% load static %}
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html> <head> <title>Hello World!</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="{% static 'style.css' %}"> </head> <body> <h1>Hello World!</h1> <p>My name is <strong>Rachit Khurana</strong></p> </body>
</html>

Now when we run the server & go to 127.0.0.1:8000/ we will see the new HTML page with the CSS.

Congratulations, you know know how to render an HTML template & use static files. But what if we need to pass an argument from the code to the template?

We can also do that easily. Let's take a look at how to do that.

Passing arguments to the template

We can easily pass variables to the template to render.

For eg, I want to pass my name & age to the template. We will go to the views.py file & declare these variables in the function.

We then need to pass another argument to the render function with all the variables we need to pass to the template as a dictionary. The final code will look like this: 
from django.shortcuts import render, HttpResponse # Create your views here.
def hello(request): name="Rachit Khurana" age=19 return render(request, 'hello.html', {'name':name, 'age':age})

Now we can use these variables in the template hello.html by wrapping them with double curly brackets like this:

{% load static %}
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html> <head> <title>Hello World!</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="{% static 'style.css' %}"> </head> <body> <h1>Hello World!</h1> <p>My name is <strong>{{name}}</strong></p> <p>My age is <strong>{{age}}</strong></p> </body>
</html>

Here {{name}} will get replaced by the value of name we have passed to the template. Now if we try to run the server & go to 127.0.0.1:8000/ we can see the new rendered HTML page with those variables.

Like this:

We can not only pass string or int values, but we can also pass lists, tuples, dictionaries etc.

Congratulations, you now know how to render templates, serve static files & pass variables to the template. But that's not all, Django templating is way more powerful than this. We will explore more about it in the next blog.

sasidhar Gadepalli - Jun 4

Once suspended, dilutewater will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, dilutewater will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by dilutewater will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If dilutewater is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag dilutewater:

Make all posts by dilutewater less visible

dilutewater consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging dilutewater will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: dilutewater

item.title: Facebook 群组访问管理建模 | Modeling Facebook Groups Access Management
item.description:
我们经常收到有关如何使用 Permify Schema Language 对不同类型的权限或策略建模的询问。

对于那些可能不熟悉的人来说，模式语言是一种授权语言，它允许您定义用户和对象之间的任意关系以对您的授权逻辑进行建模。 它由 Permify 提供，这是一种基于关系的开源授权服务。 只是想让你知道，我是Permify的维护者和联合创始人之一 :)

我们已经有文档、示例、类比和其他资源来演示我们的模式语言如何工作及其不同方面。 然而，当涉及到复杂的情况时，可能有多种方法可以达到相同的结果。 因此，用户寻求我们针对此类场景的做法和最佳实践是很正常的。

因此，我们已经开始在我们的资源中搜索和实施使用 Permify 的“现实生活”示例，我们相信这种方法可以成为推动更多采用我们的模式语言的引擎。

因此，在本教程文章中，我们将研究 Facebook Groups 授权模型并使用 Permify 实现它。

用户可以创建自己的群组或搜索现有群组以加入。 创建组时，用户定义其目的、隐私设置和其他详细信息。 该组有几种不同的用户类型，例如具有不同权限和能力的成员、管理员和版主。

每个组都有自己的供稿，成员可以在其中发布文本更新、照片、视频和其他与组主题相关的内容。 会员可以通过点赞、评论和分享来参与帖子。 群组提供其他功能，例如活动、投票、文件共享、群组聊天和协作文档，使成员能够组织活动、收集意见、共享文件和在群组内交流。

在某些时候，所有这些实体的组、帖子、评论、点赞、事件之间都具有自然联系。 例如评论，喜欢属于属于特定组的父帖子。 这种基于关系的数据结构提供了利用 ReBAC（基于关系的访问控制）创建强大访问控制结构的机会。

使用 Permify 实施

定义资源之间的实体和关系

使用 Permify 模式语言构建授权模型的第一步是创建您的实体。

因此，根据我们的 Facebook Groups 示例，我们有几个实体：

用户实体代表 Facebook 上的用户。

群组实体代表Facebook群组，它有member、admin、moderator几个关系，代表群组的成员、管理员、版主。 此外，还有表示组中帖子和评论的关系。

评论实体表示对 Facebook 群组中帖子的评论，它具有表示评论所有者、评论所属帖子和评论本身的关系。

文件实体表示Facebook群组中的一个文件，它具有表示文件所有者和文件所属群组的关系。

事件实体表示 Facebook 组中的事件，它具有表示事件所有者和事件所属组的关系。

让我们将这些实体及其关系放入我们的模式语言中，

 // Represents a user entity user {} // Represents a Facebook group entity group { // Relation to represent the members of the group relation member @user // Relation to represent the admins of the group relation admin @user // Relation to represent the moderators of the group relation moderator @user } // Represents a post in a Facebook group entity post { // Relation to represent the owner of the post relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to relation group @group } // Represents a comment on a post in a Facebook group entity comment { // Relation to represent the owner of the comment relation owner @user // Relation to represent the post that the comment belongs to relation post @post } // Represents a comment like on a post in a Facebook group entity like { // Relation to represent the owner of the like relation owner @user // Relation to represent the post that the like belongs to relation post @post } // Definition of poll entity entity poll { // Relation to represent the owner of the poll relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the poll belongs to relation group @group } // Definition of file entity entity file { // Relation to represent the owner of the file relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the file belongs to relation group @group } // Definition of event entity entity event { // Relation to represent the owner of the event relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the event belongs to relation group @group }

我们有几个附加到实体的操作，这些操作受到某些权限的限制。 让我们形成编辑帖子的权限。

要在 Facebook 群组中编辑帖子 X，用户要么是该帖子的所有者/创建者，要么用户应在帖子 X 所属的群组中具有管理员角色。

关键字 action 或 permission 可以与这些运算符一起使用来为您的授权逻辑形成规则。

因此，如果我们将编辑帖子操作指定为edit_post ，那么帖子实体应该重构如下：

 // Represents a post in a Facebook group entity post { // Relation to represent the owner of the post relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to relation group @group action edit_post = owner or group.admin }

从 group.admin 语法可以看出，您可以使用经典的点表示法访问实体关系。

让我们定义更高级的权限。 例如，我们可以在评论实体中定义一个权限“ view_comment ”来控制执行查看评论操作的权限。

在 Facebook 群组中，用户可以在以下情况下查看评论：

用户是评论帖子所属组的成员。

用户是评论的所有者/创建者。

所有权是一个基本的 ReBAC 模型，此权限的棘手部分是第一个条件的嵌套层次结构：用户是评论帖子所属组的成员。

要将此权限添加到我们的架构中，我们需要更新帖子和评论实体，如下所示：

 // Represents a post in a Facebook group entity post { .. .. // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to relation group @group // Permissions for the post entity .. .. permission group_member = group.member } // Represents a comment on a post in a Facebook group entity comment { // Relation to represent the owner of the comment relation owner @user // Relation to represent the post that the comment belongs to relation post @post relation comment @comment .. .. // Permissions action view_comment = owner or post.group_member .. .. }

我们将其定义为帖子实体中的动作，

 // Represents a post in a Facebook group entity post { .. .. // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to relation group @group // Permissions for the post entity .. .. permission group_member = group.member } // Represents a comment on a post in a Facebook group entity comment { // Relation to represent the owner of the comment relation owner @user // Relation to represent the post that the comment belongs to relation post @post relation comment @comment .. .. // Permissions action view_comment = owner or post.group_member .. .. }

权限可以作为其他实体中的关系继承。

这允许在实体之间形成嵌套的层次关系。

转向我们的示例，评论属于属于组的帖子。 由于为组实体定义了一个' member '关系，我们可以使用' group_member '权限从帖子中的组继承成员关系，然后在评论中使用它，如下所示：

 action view_comment = owner or post.group_member

完整架构

为了这篇文章的简单起见，我不会对每个权限进行分解和深入。

因此，让我们添加剩余的权限并完成我们的架构。

 // Represents a user entity user {} // Represents a Facebook group entity group { // Relation to represent the members of the group relation member @user // Relation to represent the admins of the group relation admin @user // Relation to represent the moderators of the group relation moderator @user // Permissions for the group entity action create = member action join = member action leave = member action invite_to_group = admin action remove_from_group = admin or moderator action edit_settings = admin or moderator action post_to_group = member action comment_on_post = member action view_group_insights = admin or moderator } // Represents a post in a Facebook group entity post { // Relation to represent the owner of the post relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to relation group @group // Permissions for the post entity action view_post = owner or group.member action edit_post = owner or group.admin action delete_post = owner or group.admin permission group_member = group.member } // Represents a comment on a post in a Facebook group entity comment { // Relation to represent the owner of the comment relation owner @user // Relation to represent the post that the comment belongs to relation post @post // Permissions for the comment entity action view_comment = owner or post.group_member action edit_comment = owner action delete_comment = owner } // Represents a comment like on a post in a Facebook group entity like { // Relation to represent the owner of the like relation owner @user // Relation to represent the post that the like belongs to relation post @post // Permissions for the like entity action like_post = owner or post.group_member action unlike_post = owner or post.group_member } // Definition of poll entity entity poll { // Relation to represent the owner of the poll relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the poll belongs to relation group @group // Permissions for the poll entity action create_poll = owner or group.admin action view_poll = owner or group.member action edit_poll = owner or group.admin action delete_poll = owner or group.admin } // Definition of file entity entity file { // Relation to represent the owner of the file relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the file belongs to relation group @group // Permissions for the file entity action upload_file = owner or group.member action view_file = owner or group.member action delete_file = owner or group.admin } // Definition of event entity entity event { // Relation to represent the owner of the event relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the event belongs to relation group @group // Permissions for the event entity action create_event = owner or group.admin action view_event = owner or group.member action edit_event = owner or group.admin action delete_event = owner or group.admin action RSVP_to_event = owner or group.member }

创建示例授权数据

要测试上面创建的授权模型，我们需要有相关数据。 每个数据都应表示实体、对象和用户之间的关系，并构建访问控制列表 (ACL) 的集合。

在 Permify 中，我们使用特定的形式来表示对象到对象和对象到主体的关系。 它被称为关系元组。

关系元组的最简单形式结构为实体#关系@用户。 每个关系元组表示特定用户或用户集可以对资源执行的操作，并采用用户 U 与对象 O 具有关系 R 的形式，其中用户 U 可以是简单用户或用户集，例如组 X 成员。

根据我们的 Facebook 群组示例，这里有一些关系元组，

 //group relationships group:1#member@user:1 group:1#admin@user:2 group:2#moderator@user:3 group:2#member@user:4 group:1#member@user:5 //post relationships post:1#owner@user:1 post:1#group@group:1 post:2#owner@user:4 post:2#group@group:1 //comment relationships comment:1#owner@user:2 comment:1#post@post:1 comment:2#owner@user:5 comment:2#post@post:2 //like relationships like:1#owner@user:3 like:1#post@post:1 like:2#owner@user:4 like:2#post@post:2 //poll relationships poll:1#owner@user:2 poll:1#group@group:1 poll:2#owner@user:5 poll:2#group@group:1 //like relationships file:1#owner@user:1 file:1#group@group:1 //event relationships event:1#owner@user:3 event:1#group@group:1

测试与验证

最后，我们能够检查一些权限并测试我们的授权逻辑。

让我们从事件实体中的视图动作开始，

用户 4 可以查看事件：1 吗？

 entity event { // Relation to represent the owner of the event relation owner @user // Relation to represent the group that the event belongs to relation group @group // Permissions for the event entity .. .. action view = owner or group.member }

根据我们创建的关系元组，user:4 不是事件的所有者。



原文:

We frequently receive inquiries about how to model different types of permissions or policies using Permify Schema Language.

For those who may not be familiar, the schema language is an authorization language that allows you to define arbitrary relations between users and objects to model your authorization logic. And its offered by Permify, an open-source relationship-based authorization service. Just to let you know, I'm one of the maintainers and co-founders of Permify :)

We already have documentation, examples, analogies, and other resources that demonstrate how our Schema Language works and its different aspects. Yet, when it comes to complex cases, there may be multiple approaches that can achieve the same results. So it's quite normal for users to seek our take and best practices for such scenarios.

Therefore we have started to search and implement "real life" examples that held with Permify to our resources, we believe this approach could be the engine to drive more adoption to our schema language.

So in this tutorial article, we’ll examine Facebook Groups authorization model and implement it using Permify.

Facebook Groups And Its Access Control

Facebook Groups is a feature that allows Facebook users to create and join communities centered around various interests, topics, or organizations.

Users can create their groups or search for existing groups to join. When creating a group, users define its purpose, privacy settings, and other details. There are couple of different user types of the group such as member, admin, and moderator with different permissions and abilities.

Each group has its feed where members can post text updates, photos, videos, and other content relevant to the group's topic. Members can engage with posts by liking, commenting, and sharing them. Groups provide additional features like events, polls, file sharing, group chat, and collaborative documents, enabling members to organize events, gather opinions, share files, and communicate within the group.

At some point all of these entity's group, posts, comments, likes, events have a natural linkage between them. As an example comments, likes belongs to a parent post that belongs to a specific group. This relationship-based data structure gives the opportunity to create a robust access control structure with utilizing from ReBAC (relational based access control).

Implementation with Permify

Defining Entities & Relations Between Resources

The very first step to building an authorization model with Permify schema language is creating your entities.

So according to our Facebook Groups example, we have several entities:

User entity represents a user on Facebook.

Group entity represents the Facebook group, and it has several relations including member, admin, and moderator to represent the members, admins, and moderators of the group. Additionally, there are relations to represent the posts and comments in the group.

Post entity represents a post in the Facebook group, and it has relations to represent the owner of the post and the group that the post belongs to.

Comment entity represents a comment on a post in the Facebook group, and it has relations to represent the owner of the comment, the post that the comment belongs to, and the comment itself.

Like entity represents a like on a post in the Facebook group, and it has relations to represent the owner of the like and the post that the like belongs to.

Poll entity represents a poll in the Facebook group, and it has relations to represent the owner of the poll and the group that the poll belongs to.

File entity represents a file in the Facebook group, and it has relations to represent the owner of the file and the group that the file belongs to.

Event entity represents an event in the Facebook group, and it has relations to represent the owner of the event and the group that the event belongs to.

Let's put those entities and their relations to our schema language,

// Represents a user
entity user {}

// Represents a Facebook group
entity group {

    // Relation to represent the members of the group
    relation member @user
    // Relation to represent the admins of the group
    relation admin @user
    // Relation to represent the moderators of the group
    relation moderator @user

}

// Represents a post in a Facebook group
entity post {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the post
    relation owner @user
    // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to
    relation group @group

}

// Represents a comment on a post in a Facebook group
entity comment {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the comment
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the post that the comment belongs to
    relation post @post
}

// Represents a comment like on a post in a Facebook group
entity like {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the like
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the post that the like belongs to
    relation post @post

}

// Definition of poll entity
entity poll {

     // Relation to represent the owner of the poll
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the group that the poll belongs to
    relation group @group

}

// Definition of file entity
entity file {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the file
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the group that the file belongs to
    relation group @group

}

// Definition of event entity
entity event {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the event
    relation owner @user
    // Relation to represent the group that the event belongs to
    relation group @group

}

‍Defining Permissions

We have several actions attached to the entities, which are limited by certain permissions. Let’s form permission for editing post.

To edit post X in Facebook Groups, users either be an owner/creator of that post or user should have an admin role in the group that post X belongs to.

Permify Schema language supports and, or not operators to achieve permission intersection and exclusion. The keywords action or permission can be used with those operators to form rules for your authorization logic.

So if we specify the edit post action as edit_post, then post entity should be refactored as follows:

// Represents a post in a Facebook group
entity post {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the post
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to
    relation group @group

    action edit_post = owner or group.admin

}

As you can see from the group.admin syntax, you can access entities relations with classic dot notation.

Let’s define more advanced permissions. For example, we can define a permission “view_comment" in the comment entity to control access for performing viewing comment action.

In Facebook groups, users are granted to view a comment if:

User is a member of the group which the comment's post belongs.

User is owner/creator of the comment.

Ownership is a basic ReBAC model, the tricky part of this permission is the nested hierarchical structure of first condition: user is a member of the group which the comment's post belongs.

To add this permission to our schema we need to update both the post and the comment entity as follows:

// Represents a post in a Facebook group
entity post {

    ..
    ..

    // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to
    relation group @group

    // Permissions for the post entity

    ..
    ..
    permission group_member = group.member
}

// Represents a comment on a post in a Facebook group
entity comment {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the comment
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the post that the comment belongs to
    relation post @post
    relation comment @comment

    ..
    ..

    // Permissions 
    action view_comment = owner or post.group_member

    ..
    ..
}

The post.group_member refers to the members of the group to which the post belongs. We defined it as action in post entity as,

// Represents a post in a Facebook group
entity post {

    ..
    ..

    // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to
    relation group @group

    // Permissions for the post entity

    ..
    ..
    permission group_member = group.member
}

// Represents a comment on a post in a Facebook group
entity comment {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the comment
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the post that the comment belongs to
    relation post @post
    relation comment @comment

    ..
    ..

    // Permissions 
    action view_comment = owner or post.group_member

    ..
    ..
}

Permissions can be inherited as relations in other entities.

This allows to form nested hierarchical relationships between entities.

Turning to our example, a comment belongs to a post that is part of a group. Since there is a 'member' relation defined for the group entity, we can use the 'group_member' permission to inherit the member relation from the group in the post and then use it in the comment as follows:

action view_comment = owner or post.group_member

Complete Schema

For the sake of simplicity of this post I won’t break down and go depth for each permission.

So let’s add the remaining permissions and complete our schema.

// Represents a user
entity user {}

// Represents a Facebook group
entity group {

    // Relation to represent the members of the group
    relation member @user
    // Relation to represent the admins of the group
    relation admin @user
    // Relation to represent the moderators of the group
    relation moderator @user

    // Permissions for the group entity
    action create = member
    action join = member
    action leave = member
    action invite_to_group = admin
    action remove_from_group = admin or moderator
    action edit_settings = admin or moderator
    action post_to_group = member
    action comment_on_post = member
    action view_group_insights = admin or moderator
}

// Represents a post in a Facebook group
entity post {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the post
    relation owner @user
    // Relation to represent the group that the post belongs to
    relation group @group

    // Permissions for the post entity
    action view_post = owner or group.member
    action edit_post = owner or group.admin
    action delete_post = owner or group.admin

    permission group_member = group.member
}

// Represents a comment on a post in a Facebook group
entity comment {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the comment
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the post that the comment belongs to
    relation post @post

    // Permissions for the comment entity
    action view_comment = owner or post.group_member
    action edit_comment = owner
    action delete_comment = owner
}

// Represents a comment like on a post in a Facebook group
entity like {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the like
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the post that the like belongs to
    relation post @post

    // Permissions for the like entity
    action like_post = owner or post.group_member
    action unlike_post = owner or post.group_member
}

// Definition of poll entity
entity poll {

     // Relation to represent the owner of the poll
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the group that the poll belongs to
    relation group @group

    // Permissions for the poll entity
    action create_poll = owner or group.admin
    action view_poll = owner or group.member
    action edit_poll = owner or group.admin
    action delete_poll = owner or group.admin
}

// Definition of file entity
entity file {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the file
    relation owner @user

    // Relation to represent the group that the file belongs to
    relation group @group

    // Permissions for the file entity
    action upload_file = owner or group.member
    action view_file = owner or group.member
    action delete_file = owner or group.admin
}

// Definition of event entity
entity event {

    // Relation to represent the owner of the event
    relation owner @user
    // Relation to represent the group that the event belongs to
    relation group @group

    // Permissions for the event entity
    action create_event = owner or group.admin
    action view_event = owner or group.member
    action edit_event = owner or group.admin
    action delete_event = owner or group.admin
    action RSVP_to_event = owner or group.member

}

Creating Sample Authorization Data

To test the authorization model that was created above, we need to have related data. Each data should represent relationships between entities, objects, and users and builds up a collection of access control lists (ACLs).

In Permify, we use a specific form to represent object-to-object and object-to-subject relations. It’s called relational tuples.

the simplest form of relational tuple structured as entity # relation @ user. Each relational tuple represents an action that a specific user or user set can do on a resource and takes the form of user U has relation R to object O, where user U could be a simple user or a user set such as group X members.

Here are some relational tuples according to our Facebook Groups example,

//group relationships
group:1#member@user:1
group:1#admin@user:2
group:2#moderator@user:3
group:2#member@user:4
group:1#member@user:5 //post relationships
post:1#owner@user:1
post:1#group@group:1
post:2#owner@user:4
post:2#group@group:1 //comment relationships
comment:1#owner@user:2
comment:1#post@post:1
comment:2#owner@user:5
comment:2#post@post:2 //like relationships
like:1#owner@user:3
like:1#post@post:1
like:2#owner@user:4
like:2#post@post:2 //poll relationships
poll:1#owner@user:2
poll:1#group@group:1
poll:2#owner@user:5
poll:2#group@group:1 //like relationships
file:1#owner@user:1
file:1#group@group:1 //event relationships
event:1#owner@user:3
event:1#group@group:1

Test & Validation

Finally, we are able to check some permissions and test our authorization logic.

Let's start with the view action in the event entity,

can user 4 view event:1 ? 

entity event {

   // Relation to represent the owner of the event
   relation owner @user
   // Relation to represent the group that the event belongs to

   relation group @group

   // Permissions for the event entity

   ..
   ..

   action view = owner or group.member
}

‍According to what we have defined for the 'view' action, users who are either the owner of event:1 or a member of the group that belongs to event:1 can view the event.  

According to the relation tuples we created, user:4 is not the owner of the event.
item.author: egeaytin

item.title: 使用死信队列 (DLQ) 进行故障设计 | Design for failure by using Dead Letter Queues (DLQ)
item.description:
什么是死信以及为什么它们在任何分布式系统中都是必不可少的

什么是死信

让我们从一个简短的定义开始，它在分布式系统和消息传递平台的上下文中有效：

死信只是一个容器，用于收集和存储无法传递的消息。

这个容器通常是一个队列，但不是严格强制的，概念更广泛，不一定基于它。

本文不关注特定的经纪商或技术，而是从一般角度探讨该主题。 每个系统都有其特殊性，官方文档绝对是最佳选择。

关于名字的简短题外话

现实世界中的邮件传递与分布式系统中软件组件之间的消息传递方式有很强的相似性。 在现实世界中，一封信件或包裹被标记为“无法投递”的原因有很多：收件人和发件人地址均不正确，信封已损坏且地址不再可读，收件人和发件人均已不可用，内容不符合邮政法规，等等。 不同国家的所有邮政公司都面临着处理所有无法投递邮件的问题，通常有专门的办公室负责打开私人信件和包裹，寻找收件人或发件人的线索，最终能够投递或转向销毁或拍卖内容的极端解决方案。

如果你喜欢花一些时间阅读有趣的事实，可以考虑用谷歌搜索世界各地死信办公室的故事和统计数据，你会发现许多惊人的数字和关于奇怪内容的有趣故事，从活着的响尾蛇到发臭的死鱼，人类头骨、装满钱、毒品和武器的袋子。

失败设计

回到分布式系统领域，当消息系统负责组件间的异步通信时，提供一种机制来处理故障是必不可少的。 总的来说，这是一个很好的建议。 正如 Amazon CTO Werner Vogels 明智地说的那样：“一切都会失败，一直都是”。

换句话说，关键不在于是否会失败，而在于何时会发生。

因此，我们需要设计系统，以便它们在这些可预见的故障期间和之后尽可能继续工作。

如何处理错误

任何异步通信都可能遇到与交付相关的不同类型的问题：

  • 收件人不在
  • 收件人明确拒绝邮件
  • 收件人未确认消息

该问题可能是暂时的，也可能是持久的，因此应相应地改变后续行动。

但是当发生交付失败时，两个组件之间的代理只有几个选择：

  • 再试一次
  • 锁定队列
  • 丢弃消息并继续

选项 1：重试

对于暂时性错误，重试是一个不错的选择，但如果错误持续存在，则在不会适得其反的情况下是无用的。

重试应该在每次尝试之间等待一段时间，通过指数退避算法。 在这种情况下，重试过程可能需要很长时间，这实际上会导致临时锁定，这对于高负载系统来说是不可接受的，如下一个要点所述。

此外，即使是暂时性错误，如果重复多次，在实践中也应被视为持续性错误。 无限循环从来都不是一个好主意，并且在某个时刻，无论如何，如果它不起作用，建议停止尝试。

我们可以说一般来说重试是好的，但通常是不够的。

选项 2：锁定

当对消息排序有严格的约束时，锁定队列的选项是强制性的，但幸运的是，这不是最常见的情况。 在大多数情况下，分布式系统旨在处理无序消息，而毒消息可以简单地放在一边以供重试或未来调查。 锁定有时是一个合理的选择，但在很多情况下，尤其是当有一个关键进程需要处理大量数据时，它根本不可接受。

一个锁定的队列确实意味着队列在无限增长，这是不可持续的，并且会带来其他更糟糕的基础设施问题。 除此之外，消费者不允许在锁定的队列上继续操作，这意味着某人（用户或软件）没有收到预期的数据，遇到故障或意外延迟。

选项 3：放弃并继续

这里是第三种选择：丢弃消息并继续处理其他消息。 这里的“discard”并不是删除消息的意思，而是“从队列中移除并放到某个地方”的意思。

这通常是最好的选择，因为消费者可以忘记有问题的消息并继续工作，但消息不会丢失并且可以在以后进行分析和/或恢复。

死信队列与其他选项

死信消息的一般概念可以通过多种方式进行。

仅举几例，毒消息可以简单地保存到日志文件中，可以插入数据库中，可以以文本文件的形式写入磁盘，或者可以保存到对象存储服务中，如 AWS S3 或Azure Blob 存储。

在某些特定情况下，其中一种策略甚至可能是最佳选择，但使用死信队列有很多优势。

更快的重试和清除

死信队列毕竟是一个普通队列，具有这种实体提供的所有优点。 例如，通常很容易将来自 DLQ 的消息“转发”到原始队列。 例如，如果消费者有一个错误同时已得到纠正，我们可以简单地重新排队死信消息，新版本的消费者现在将能够处理“旧”死信消息实时消息的普通流。

相反，如果经过调查我们知道这些消息是错误的并且可以删除，那么只需单击一下我们就可以清除整个队列。

免费的可观察性

如果您正在使用消息代理，您可能已经有了一个监控平台。 在这种情况下，几乎可以立即在 DLQ 上激活可观察性原则，只需很少的额外工作。 无需在您的代码中生成自定义指标或分析日志文件来理解和计算错误，因为如果配置正确，代理可以为您完成。

与主队列的速率相比，消息进入 DLQ 的速率是衡量消费者运行的进程健康状况的良好指标。 死信中消息的绝对数量告诉我们自上次清除以来我们有多少次失败，如果速率具有可预测的趋势或 DLQ 通常为空，这些指标也是自动警报的不错选择。

没有自定义代码

大多数经纪人在向死信发送消息时，会在消息的标题中添加一个字段，说明发生这种情况的原因。 它可能是代理生成的错误，如“重试次数太多”或“消息已过期”，或者可能是消费者生成的错误和代理转发的错误，如“明确拒绝此错误消息：未知 ID”。

死信的原因放在消息本身中，这是免费的，无需编写代码，因为它是在代理中实现的，而不是在消费者软件中。

不太适合分析

与数据库等其他解决方案相比，我在使用 DLQ 时遇到的唯一小缺点是队列中的消息无法轻松查询和计数，只能一条一条地查看。 如果大量消息以死信结尾，则最好附加一个消费者并将它们移动到可以对其进行统计分析的数据存储中。

死信队列绝对是保存错误消息和毒消息的最佳场所。 监控更丰富、更容易，重新发布和清除非常快，经纪人自己负责这个过程。

一旦暂停，ctaverna 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，ctaverna 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ctaverna 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ctaverna 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 ctaverna：

降低 ctaverna 发布的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 ctaverna 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

What are dead letters and why they are essential in any distributed system

What is a dead letter

Let's start with a short definition, effective in the context of distributed systems and messaging platforms:

A dead letter is simply a container where messages that cannot be delivered are collected and stored.

This container is usually a queue, but it's not strictly mandatory, the concept is broader and not necessarily based on it.

This article is not focused on a specific broker or technology, but addresses the subject from a general perspective. Each system has its specificities, and the official documentation is definitely the way to go.

A short off-topic about the name

The term "dead letter" is due to an expression borrowed by the postal world. There are strong analogies between real-world mail deliveries and how, in distributed systems, messages are delivered between software components. In the real world, there are many reasons for a letter or a package to be marked as "undeliverable": both recipient and sender addresses are incorrect, the envelope is damaged and addresses are no more readable, both recipient and sender are no more available, the content is not compliant with postal regulations, and many others. All postal companies in different countries have faced the problem of handling all undeliverable mail, usually with a dedicated office having the critical responsibility of opening private letters and packages, searching for clues about the recipient or the sender, to finally becoming able to deliver it or move to the extreme solution of destroying or auctioning the content.

If you like to waste some time reading fun facts, consider googling about the story and the statistics of Dead Letter Offices all around the world, you'll find many astonishing numbers and funny stories about strange contents, from alive rattlesnakes to smelly dead fishes, human skulls, bags full of money, drugs, and weapons.

Design for failure

Jumping back to the field of distributed systems, when a messaging system has the responsibility of the asynchronous communication between components, it's essential to provide a mechanism to handle the failures. This is, in general, a piece of good advice. Like the Amazon CTO Werner Vogels has wisely said: "Everything fails, all the time".

In other words, the point is not IF something will fail, but only WHEN this will happen.

Hence we need to design systems so that they will continue to work, as much as possible, also during and after these foreseeable failures.

How to handle errors

Any asynchronous communication can potentially encounter different kinds of problems related to delivery:

  • The recipient is not available
  • The recipient explicitly refuses the message
  • The recipient is not giving an acknowledge to the message

The problem can be transient or persistent, and the consequent action should change accordingly.

But when a delivery failure happens, the broker between two components has only a few options:

  • Try again
  • Lock the queue
  • Discard the message and continue

Option 1: Retry

Retrying is a good option for transient errors, but it is useless, when not counterproductive, if the error is persistent.

Retrying should be done waiting some time between each attempt, through an exponential backoff algorithm. In this case, the retry process can take a long time and this results actually in a temporary lock, which can be unacceptable for high-load systems, as discussed in the next bullet point.

Also, even a transient error, if repeated many times, should be considered in practice a persistent error. An infinite loop is never a good idea, and at a certain point, in any case, it is advisable to stop trying if it is not working.

We can say that generally speaking retrying is good, but it's usually not enough.

Option 2: Lock

The option of locking the queue is mandatory when there is a strict constraint about message ordering, but fortunately, this is not the most common scenario. In most cases the distributed systems are designed to handle unordered messages, and poison messages can simply be put aside for retry or future investigation. Locking can sometimes be a reasonable option, but in many cases, especially when there is a critical process with a huge quantity of data to be processed, it is simply not acceptable.

A locked queue means indeed that the queue is growing indefinitely, and this is not sustainable for a long time, and can bring other even worse infrastructure problems. In addition to that, the consumers are not allowed to proceed on a locked queue, and this means that someone (user or software) is not receiving the expected data, experiencing a malfunction or an unexpected delay.

Option 3: Discard and continue

And here we are to the third option: discard the message and continue to process the others. Here, "discard" doesn't mean to delete the message but means "remove from the queue and put it somewhere".

This is usually the best option because the consumer can forget the problematic message and continue to work, but the message is not lost and can be analyzed and/or recovered later on.

Dead letter queue vs other options

The general concept of dead lettering a message can be carried on in multiple ways.

Just to list a few, the poison messages could be simply saved into log files, it could be inserted in a database, it could be written to disk in a text file, or it could be saved into an object storage service like AWS S3 or Azure Blob Storage.

In some specific cases, one of these strategies might even be the best option, but using a dead letter queue has many advantages.

Faster retry and purging

A dead letter queue is, after all, a normal queue, with all the advantages offered by this kind of entity. For example, it's usually very easy to "forward" the messages from the DLQ to the original queue. If the consumer had a bug that meanwhile has been corrected, for example, we can simply re-enqueue the dead-lettered messages, and the new version of the consumer will be now able to process the "old" dead-lettered messages together with the ordinary flow of real-time messages.

On the contrary, if after an investigation we know that the messages are wrong and can be deleted, with one click we can purge the entire queue.

Observability for free

If you are using a message broker you likely already have a monitoring platform. In this case, it will be almost immediate to activate observability principles also on the DLQ, with very little additional effort. No need to generate custom metrics in your code or analyze log files to understand and count errors, because the broker, if configured properly, can do it for you.

The rate of messages entering the DLQ, compared with the rate of the main queue, is a good metric for the health status of the process run by the consumers. The absolute quantity of messages in the dead letter is telling us how many failures we had since the last purge, and if the rate has a predictable trend or DLQ is usually empty, these metrics are also good choices for automatic alerts.

No custom code

Most brokers, when sending a message to the dead letter, add in the header of the message a field with the reason why this has happened. It may be a broker-generated error like "too many retries" or "message expired", or maybe a consumer-generated and broker-forwarded error like "explicitly nacked with this error message: unknown id".

The reason for dead-lettering is put inside the message itself, and this comes for free, without writing code, because it is implemented in the broker, not in the consumer software.

Not so good for analysis

The only little disadvantage that I experienced using DLQs, compared to other solutions like a database, is the fact that messages in a queue cannot be easily queried and counted, but can only be peeked one by one. In case of high volumes and considerable quantities of messages ending up in a dead letter, it can be a good idea to attach a consumer and move them into a data store where it is possible to analyze them statistically.

TLDR

Dead letter queues are definitely the best place to save error messages and poison messages. Monitoring is richer and easier, re-publishing and purging is super fast and the broker itself owns the responsibility of this process.

Once suspended, ctaverna will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ctaverna will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ctaverna will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ctaverna is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ctaverna:

Make all posts by ctaverna less visible

ctaverna consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ctaverna will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ctaverna

item.title: 构建用于图像标签检测的无服务器应用程序 | Build a Serverless Application for Image Label Detection
item.description:
在这篇博文中，您将学习如何使用Amazon Rekognition 、 AWS Lambda和Go编程语言构建无服务器解决方案来处理图像。 上传到 Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) 的图像将触发 Lambda 函数，该函数将检测标签（使用 AWS Go SDK ）并将图像标签数据保存到 Amazon DynamoDB 表中。

您将为业务逻辑使用Go编程语言（感谢aws-lambda-go库）以及基础设施组件（ AWS CDK 的 Go 绑定）来部署解决方案。

代码在GitHub上可用

覆盖了什么？

介绍

您可以识别对象、人物、文本、场景和活动，并检测不当内容。 您还可以针对用户验证和公共安全等各种用例进行面部分析、面部比较和面部搜索。 它有一个易于使用的 API，可以分析 Amazon S3 中的任何图像或视频文件。

使用 Amazon Rekognition 的常见用例包括：

  • 使图像和视频可搜索 - 发现其中出现的对象和场景。
  • 基于人脸的用户验证 - 通过将用户的实时图像与参考图像进行比较来确认用户身份。
  • 情绪和人口统计分析——解释情绪表达，例如快乐、悲伤或惊讶，以及人口统计信息。
  • 面部搜索 - 搜索图像、存储的视频和流媒体视频，查找与存储在称为面部集合的容器中相匹配的面部。
  • 不安全内容检测——检测图像和存储视频中的成人和暴力内容，并使用返回的元数据根据业务需求过滤不当内容。 文本检测 - 用于根据车牌号进行视觉搜索、编目和识别车辆

让我们通过动手教程学习 Amazon Rekognition。

先决条件

在继续之前，请确保您已安装以下内容：

  • 命令行界面

克隆项目并切换到正确的目录：

 git clone https://github.com/abhirockzz/ai-ml-golang-rekognition-label-detection cd ai-ml-golang-rekognition-label-detection

使用 AWS CDK 部署解决方案

要开始部署，只需调用cdk deploy并稍等片刻。 您将看到将要创建的资源列表，需要您确认才能继续。

 cd cdk cdk deploy # output Bundling asset RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack/rekognition-function/Code/Stage... ✨ Synthesis time: 5.44 //.... omitted Do you wish to deploy these changes (y/n)? y

输入y开始创建应用程序所需的 AWS 资源。

如果您想查看将在幕后使用的 AWS CloudFormation 模板，请运行cdk synth并检查cdk.out文件夹

您可以在终端中跟踪堆栈创建进度或导航至 AWS 控制台： CloudFormation > Stacks > RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack 。

堆栈创建完成后，您应该：

  • 使用 Amazon Rekognition 提取图像标签的 Lambda 函数。
  • 用于存储每个图像的标签数据的DyanmoDB表。
  • 您还将在终端中看到以下输出（资源名称因您的情况而异）。 在这种情况下，这些是 CDK 创建的 S3 存储桶的名称：

     ✅ RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack ✨ Deployment time: 119.56s Outputs: RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack.abeldetectionoutputtablename = rekognitionlabeldetectio-labeldetectioninputbucke-v3vn9o06q3kb_labels_output RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack.labeldetectioninputbucketname = rekognitionlabeldetectio-labeldetectioninputbucke-v3vn9o06q3kb .....

    您现在可以尝试端到端的解决方案！

    从图像中提取标签

    要尝试该解决方案，您可以使用自己的图像或使用GitHub 存储库中提供的示例文件。 我将使用 S3 CLI 上传文件，但您也可以使用 AWS 控制台。

     export SOURCE_BUCKET=<enter source S3 bucket name - check the CDK output> aws s3 cp ./car.png s3://$SOURCE_BUCKET # verify that the file was uploaded aws s3 ls s3://$SOURCE_BUCKET

    此 Lambda 函数将从图像中提取标签并将它们存储在 DynamoDB 表中。

    上传另一个文件：

     export SOURCE_BUCKET=<enter source S3 bucket name - check the CDK output> aws s3 cp ./skate.png s3://$SOURCE_BUCKET

    检查 AWS 控制台中的 DynamoDB 表 - 您应该看到两个图像的标签检测结果。

    这允许几个查询模式：

    • 您可以获得给定图像的所有标签。
    • 您可以查询特定源图像及其标签的元数据（类别和置信度）。

    您还可以使用 CLI 扫描表：

     aws dynamodb scan --table-name <enter table name - check the CDK output>

    不要忘记清理

    完成后，要删除所有服务，只需使用：

     cdk destroy #output prompt (choose 'y' to continue) Are you sure you want to delete: RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack (y/n)?

    您能够设置并尝试完整的解决方案。 在结束之前，让我们快速浏览一下代码的一些重要部分，以便更好地了解幕后发生的事情。

    代码遍历

    我们将只关注重要的部分——为简洁起见，省略了一些代码。

    完整的CDK代码可以参考这里

    bucket := awss3.NewBucket(stack, jsii.String("label-detection-input-bucket"), &awss3.BucketProps{ BlockPublicAccess: awss3.BlockPublicAccess_BLOCK_ALL(), RemovalPolicy: awscdk.RemovalPolicy_DESTROY, AutoDeleteObjects: jsii.Bool(true), })

    我们首先创建源 S3 存储桶。

     table := awsdynamodb.NewTable(stack, jsii.String("label-detection-output-table"), &awsdynamodb.TableProps{ PartitionKey: &awsdynamodb.Attribute{ Name: jsii.String("source_file"), Type: awsdynamodb.AttributeType_STRING}, SortKey: &awsdynamodb.Attribute{ Name: jsii.String("label_name"), Type: awsdynamodb.AttributeType_STRING}, TableName: jsii.String(*bucket.BucketName() + "_labels_output"), })

    然后，我们创建一个 DynamoDB 表来存储每个图像的标签数据。

     function := awscdklambdagoalpha.NewGoFunction(stack, jsii.String("rekognition-function"), &awscdklambdagoalpha.GoFunctionProps{ Runtime: awslambda.Runtime_GO_1_X(), Environment: &map[string]*string{"TABLE_NAME": table.TableName()}, Entry: jsii.String(functionDir), }) table.GrantWriteData(function) function.Role().AddManagedPolicy(awsiam.ManagedPolicy_FromAwsManagedPolicyName(jsii.String("AmazonRekognitionReadOnlyAccess"))) bucket.GrantRead(function, "*")

    接下来，我们创建 Lambda 函数，将DynamoDB表名称作为环境变量传递给该函数。 我们还授予函数访问DynamoDB表和 S3 存储桶的权限。 我们还授予函数访问AmazonRekognitionReadOnlyAccess托管策略的权限。

     function.AddEventSource(awslambdaeventsources.NewS3EventSource(sourceBucket, &awslambdaeventsources.S3EventSourceProps{ Events: &[]awss3.EventType{awss3.EventType_OBJECT_CREATED}, }))

    我们向 Lambda 函数添​​加一个事件源，以在新文件上传到源存储桶时触发它。

     awscdk.NewCfnOutput(stack, jsii.String("label-detection-input-bucket-name"), &awscdk.CfnOutputProps{ ExportName: jsii.String("label-detection-input-bucket-name"), Value: bucket.BucketName()}) awscdk.NewCfnOutput(stack, jsii.String("label-detection-output-table-name"), &awscdk.CfnOutputProps{ ExportName: jsii.String("label-detection-output-table-name"), Value: table.TableName()})

    最后，我们将存储桶和 DynamoDB 表名称导出为 CloudFormation 输出。

    拉姆达函数

    您可以 在此处参考完整的 Lambda 函数代码

    func handler(ctx context.Context, s3Event events.S3Event) { for _, record := range s3Event.Records { sourceBucketName := record.S3.Bucket.Name fileName := record.S3.Object.Key err := labelDetection(sourceBucketName, fileName) } }

    将新图像上传到源存储桶时会触发 Lambda 函数。 该函数遍历文件列表并为每个图像调用labelDetection函数。

    让我们通过它。

     func labelDetection(sourceBucketName, fileName string) error { resp, err := rekognitionClient.DetectLabels(context.Background(), &rekognition.DetectLabelsInput{ Image: &types.Image{ S3Object: &types.S3Object{ Bucket: aws.String(sourceBucketName), Name: aws.String(fileName), }, }, }) for _, label := range resp.Labels { item := make(map[string]ddbTypes.AttributeValue) item["source_file"] = &ddbTypes.AttributeValueMemberS{Value: fileName} item["label_name"] = &ddbTypes.AttributeValueMemberS{Value: *label.Name} item["label_category"] = &ddbTypes.AttributeValueMemberS{Value: *label.Categories[0].Name} item["label_confidence"] = &ddbTypes.AttributeValueMemberN{Value: fmt.Sprintf("%v", aws.ToFloat32(label.Confidence))} _, err := dynamodbClient.PutItem(context.Background(), &dynamodb.PutItemInput{ TableName: aws.String(table), Item: item, }) } return nil }
    • 该函数遍历标签列表并将标签名称、类别和置信度分数存储在 DynamoDB 表中。

    结论和后续步骤

    在本文中，您了解了如何使用 Amazon Rekognition 创建检测图像标签的无服务器解决方案。 整个基础设施生命周期是使用 AWS CDK 实现自动化的。 所有这些都是使用 Go 编程语言完成的，该语言在AWS Lambda和AWS CDK中得到了很好的支持。

    您可以尝试以下几项来扩展此解决方案：

    快乐的建筑！



原文:

In this blog post, you will learn how to build a Serverless solution to process images using Amazon Rekognition, AWS Lambda and the Go programming language. Images uploaded to Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) will trigger a Lambda function which will detect labels (using the AWS Go SDK) and persist the image label data to an Amazon DynamoDB table.

You will be using the Go programming language for the business logic (thanks to aws-lambda-go library) as well as the infrastructure component (Go bindings for AWS CDK) to deploy the solution.

The code is available on GitHub

What's covered?

Introduction

Amazon Rekognition is a service that lets you analyze images and videos in your applications. You can identify objects, people, text, scenes, and activities, and detect inappropriate content. You can also do facial analysis, face comparison, and face search for various use cases like user verification and public safety. Amazon Rekognition is built on deep learning technology that doesn't require machine learning expertise to use. It has an easy-to-use API that can analyze any image or video file in Amazon S3.

Common use cases for using Amazon Rekognition include:

  • Making images and videos searchable - Discover objects and scenes that appear within them.
  • Face-based user verification - Confirm user identities by comparing their live image with a reference image.
  • Sentiment and demographic analysis - Interpret emotional expressions such as happy, sad, or surprise, and demographic information.
  • Facial search - Search images, stored videos, and streaming videos for faces that match those stored in a container known as a face collection.
  • Unsafe content detection - Detect adult and violent content in images and in stored videos and use the returned metadata to filter inappropriate content based on business needs. Text detection - Used for visual search, cataloging, and identifying vehicles based on license plate numbers

Let's learn Amazon Rekognition with a hands-on tutorial.

Pre-requisites

Before you proceed, make sure you have the following installed:

  • Go programming language (v1.18 or higher)
  • AWS CDK
  • AWS CLI

Clone the project and change to the right directory:

git clone https://github.com/abhirockzz/ai-ml-golang-rekognition-label-detection cd ai-ml-golang-rekognition-label-detection

Use AWS CDK to deploy the solution

The AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK) is a framework that lets you define your cloud infrastructure as code in one of its supported programming and provision it through AWS CloudFormation.

To start the deployment, simply invoke cdk deploy and wait for a bit. You will see a list of resources that will be created and will need to provide your confirmation to proceed.

cd cdk cdk deploy # output Bundling asset RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack/rekognition-function/Code/Stage... ✨ Synthesis time: 5.44 //.... omitted Do you wish to deploy these changes (y/n)? y

Enter y to start creating the AWS resources required for the application.

If you want to see the AWS CloudFormation template which will be used behind the scenes, run cdk synth and check the cdk.out folder

You can keep track of the stack creation progress in the terminal or navigate to AWS console: CloudFormation > Stacks > RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack.

Once the stack creation is complete, you should have:

  • An S3 bucket - Source bucket to upload images.
  • A Lambda function to extract image labels using Amazon Rekognition.
  • A DyanmoDB table to store the label data for each image.
  • .... along with a few other components (like IAM roles etc.)

You will also see the following output in the terminal (resource names will differ in your case). In this case, these are the names of the S3 buckets created by CDK:

 ✅ RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack ✨ Deployment time: 119.56s Outputs:
RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack.abeldetectionoutputtablename = rekognitionlabeldetectio-labeldetectioninputbucke-v3vn9o06q3kb_labels_output
RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack.labeldetectioninputbucketname = rekognitionlabeldetectio-labeldetectioninputbucke-v3vn9o06q3kb
.....

You can now try out the end to end solution!

Extract labels from image

To try the solution, you can either use an image of your own or use the sample files provided in the GitHub repository. I will be used the S3 CLI to upload the file, but you can use the AWS console as well.

export SOURCE_BUCKET=<enter source S3 bucket name - check the CDK output> aws s3 cp ./car.png s3://$SOURCE_BUCKET # verify that the file was uploaded
aws s3 ls s3://$SOURCE_BUCKET

This Lambda function will extract labels from the image and store them in a DynamoDB table.

Upload another file:

export SOURCE_BUCKET=<enter source S3 bucket name - check the CDK output> aws s3 cp ./skate.png s3://$SOURCE_BUCKET

Check the DynamoDB table in the AWS console - you should see results of the label detection for both the images.

DynamoDB table is designed with source file name as the partition key and (detected) label name as the sort key. This allows for a couple of query patterns:

  • You can get all the labels for a given image.
  • You can query for the metadata (category and confidence) for a specific source image and it's label.

You can also use the CLI to scan the table:

aws dynamodb scan --table-name <enter table name - check the CDK output>

Don't forget to clean up

Once you're done, to delete all the services, simply use:

cdk destroy #output prompt (choose 'y' to continue) Are you sure you want to delete: RekognitionLabelDetectionGolangStack (y/n)?

You were able to setup and try the complete solution. Before we wrap up, let's quickly walk through some of important parts of the code to get a better understanding of what's going on behind the scenes.

Code walk through

We will only focus on the important parts - some of the code has been omitted for brevity.

CDK

You can refer to the complete CDK code here

bucket := awss3.NewBucket(stack, jsii.String("label-detection-input-bucket"), &awss3.BucketProps{ BlockPublicAccess: awss3.BlockPublicAccess_BLOCK_ALL(), RemovalPolicy: awscdk.RemovalPolicy_DESTROY, AutoDeleteObjects: jsii.Bool(true), })

We start by creating the source S3 bucket.

 table := awsdynamodb.NewTable(stack, jsii.String("label-detection-output-table"), &awsdynamodb.TableProps{ PartitionKey: &awsdynamodb.Attribute{ Name: jsii.String("source_file"), Type: awsdynamodb.AttributeType_STRING}, SortKey: &awsdynamodb.Attribute{ Name: jsii.String("label_name"), Type: awsdynamodb.AttributeType_STRING}, TableName: jsii.String(*bucket.BucketName() + "_labels_output"), })

Then, we create a DynamoDB table to store the label data for each image.

function := awscdklambdagoalpha.NewGoFunction(stack, jsii.String("rekognition-function"), &awscdklambdagoalpha.GoFunctionProps{ Runtime: awslambda.Runtime_GO_1_X(), Environment: &map[string]*string{"TABLE_NAME": table.TableName()}, Entry: jsii.String(functionDir), }) table.GrantWriteData(function) function.Role().AddManagedPolicy(awsiam.ManagedPolicy_FromAwsManagedPolicyName(jsii.String("AmazonRekognitionReadOnlyAccess"))) bucket.GrantRead(function, "*")

Next, we create the Lambda function, passing the DynamoDB table name as an environment variable to the function. We also grant the function access to the DynamoDB table and the S3 bucket. We also grant the function access to the AmazonRekognitionReadOnlyAccess managed policy.

function.AddEventSource(awslambdaeventsources.NewS3EventSource(sourceBucket, &awslambdaeventsources.S3EventSourceProps{ Events: &[]awss3.EventType{awss3.EventType_OBJECT_CREATED}, }))

We add an event source to the Lambda function to trigger it when a new file is uploaded to the source bucket.

 awscdk.NewCfnOutput(stack, jsii.String("label-detection-input-bucket-name"), &awscdk.CfnOutputProps{ ExportName: jsii.String("label-detection-input-bucket-name"), Value: bucket.BucketName()}) awscdk.NewCfnOutput(stack, jsii.String("label-detection-output-table-name"), &awscdk.CfnOutputProps{ ExportName: jsii.String("label-detection-output-table-name"), Value: table.TableName()})

Finally, we export the bucket and DynamoDB table names as CloudFormation output.

Lambda function

You can refer to the complete Lambda Function code here

func handler(ctx context.Context, s3Event events.S3Event) { for _, record := range s3Event.Records { sourceBucketName := record.S3.Bucket.Name fileName := record.S3.Object.Key err := labelDetection(sourceBucketName, fileName) }
}

The Lambda function is triggered when a new image is uploaded to the source bucket. The function iterates through the list of files and calls the labelDetection function for each image.

Let's go through it.

func labelDetection(sourceBucketName, fileName string) error { resp, err := rekognitionClient.DetectLabels(context.Background(), &rekognition.DetectLabelsInput{ Image: &types.Image{ S3Object: &types.S3Object{ Bucket: aws.String(sourceBucketName), Name: aws.String(fileName), }, }, }) for _, label := range resp.Labels { item := make(map[string]ddbTypes.AttributeValue) item["source_file"] = &ddbTypes.AttributeValueMemberS{Value: fileName} item["label_name"] = &ddbTypes.AttributeValueMemberS{Value: *label.Name} item["label_category"] = &ddbTypes.AttributeValueMemberS{Value: *label.Categories[0].Name} item["label_confidence"] = &ddbTypes.AttributeValueMemberN{Value: fmt.Sprintf("%v", aws.ToFloat32(label.Confidence))} _, err := dynamodbClient.PutItem(context.Background(), &dynamodb.PutItemInput{ TableName: aws.String(table), Item: item, }) } return nil
}
  • The labelDetection function uses the DetectLabels API to detect labels in the image.
  • The API returns a list of labels, each with a confidence score.
  • The function iterates through the list of labels and stores the label name, category and confidence score in the DynamoDB table.

Conclusion and next steps

In this post, you saw how to create a serverless solution that detect image labels using Amazon Rekognition. The entire infrastructure life-cycle was automated using AWS CDK. All this was done using the Go programming language, which is well supported in AWS Lambda and AWS CDK.

Here are a few things you can try out to extend this solution:

Happy building!
item.author: abhirockzz

item.title: 远程团队的最佳软件工具 | Best Software Tools for Remote Teams
item.description:
在当今快速发展的工作环境中，远程团队已成为新常态。 由于团队成员分布在不同的地点，因此拥有合适的软件工具以确保无缝协作、生产力和有效沟通至关重要。 在这篇博文中，我们将探索适用于远程团队的最佳软件工具，这些工具可以提高他们的效率并简化他们的工作流程。

雷莫蒂克斯


在远程访问计算机方面，Remotix 是一款首选软件工具。 这个功能强大的应用程序使团队成员能够安全地连接到他们的办公室计算机或其他设备，提供从任何地方对文件、程序和资源的无缝访问。 此外，Remotix 还提供监控功能，使团队领导能够监督团队成员的活动并确保最佳生产力。

银幕


在虚拟会议和协作会议期间进行屏幕共享时，Coscreen 是一流的工具。 凭借其友好的用户界面和强大的功能，Coscreen 允许团队成员毫不费力地共享他们的屏幕，从而更轻松地表达想法、提供实时反馈以及共同处理项目。 该工具可帮助远程团队培养凝聚力和生产力，即使在距离遥远的情况下也是如此。

米兰诺特


视觉协作对于从事创意项目或头脑风暴会议的远程团队至关重要。 借助其灵活的画布，用户可以创建看板、添加图像、注释和多媒体元素，从而为远程团队打造动态且具有视觉吸引力的工作区。

何时何地


全球远程团队经常面临时区差异的挑战。 团队成员可以轻松地将会议时间或项目截止日期转换为当地时区，确保每个人都在同一页面上并避免日程安排冲突。

为了重现实体办公室的体验，Wurkr.io 为远程团队提供了一个虚拟工作空间。 该平台提供了一个共享的数字环境，团队成员可以在其中实时聚集、互动和协作。 从虚拟会议室到可定制的工作区，Wurkr.io 增强了团队沟通、参与度和生产力。

结论

远程团队的成功在很大程度上依赖于能够促进有效沟通、协作和生产力的正确软件工具集。 这篇文章中提到的软件工具可满足远程团队工作流程的各个方面，从屏幕共享和虚拟工作空间到时间跟踪和内容审查。 通过利用这些工具，远程团队可以克服距离的挑战并无缝协作，尽管在物理上相距甚远，但仍能取得显著成果。



原文:

In today's rapidly evolving work landscape, remote teams have become the new norm. With team members spread across different locations, it's crucial to have the right software tools in place to ensure seamless collaboration, productivity, and effective communication. In this blog post, we will explore the best software tools for remote teams that can enhance their efficiency and streamline their workflows.

Remotix


When it comes to accessing computers remotely, Remotix is a go-to software tool. This powerful application enables team members to securely connect to their office computers or other devices, providing seamless access to files, programs, and resources from anywhere. Additionally, Remotix offers monitoring capabilities, allowing team leaders to oversee team members' activities and ensure optimal productivity.

Coscreen


Coscreen is a top-notch tool When it comes to screen sharing during virtual meetings and collaborative sessions. With its user-friendly interface and robust features, Coscreen allows team members to effortlessly share their screens, making it easier to present ideas, provide real-time feedback, and work on projects together. This tool helps remote teams foster a sense of cohesion and productivity, even when physically distant.

Milanote


Visual collaboration is essential for remote teams working on creative projects or brainstorming sessions. Milanote is an intuitive tool that enables teams to visually organize their ideas, inspirations, and project assets. With its flexible canvas, users can create boards, add images, notes, and multimedia elements, fostering a dynamic and visually appealing workspace for remote teams.

Whena.re


Global remote teams often face challenges with time zone differences. Whena.re helps alleviate this problem by providing a simple and efficient time converter. Team members can easily convert meeting times or project deadlines to their local time zones, ensuring everyone is on the same page and avoiding scheduling conflicts.

Wurkr.io


To recreate the experience of a physical office, Wurkr.io provides a virtual workspace for remote teams. This platform offers a shared digital environment where team members can gather, interact, and collaborate in real-time. From virtual meeting rooms to customizable workspaces, Wurkr.io enhances team communication, engagement, and productivity.

Conclusion

The success of remote teams heavily relies on the right set of software tools that promote effective communication, collaboration, and productivity. The software tools mentioned in this post cater to various aspects of remote team workflows, from screen sharing and virtual workspaces to time tracking and content review. By leveraging these tools, remote teams can overcome the challenges of distance and work together seamlessly, achieving remarkable results despite being physically apart.
item.author: elliot_brenya

item.title: 在项目期间更换计算机：最佳实践 | Changing computers during a project: best practices
item.description:
在今天的工作环境中，远程工作和灵活的时间表很普遍，我们开发人员经常发现自己在多台计算机之间切换。 有时它会变得非常混乱，比如试图解开背包中的一堆充电线。 但不要害怕，因为在本文中我们将探讨一些策略和最佳实践，使整个计算机切换过程像热煎饼上的黄油一样顺滑。

开发环境的一致性

切换计算机可能会很麻烦，但如果您想让转换更容易，那么在您的开发环境中建立某种一致性是至关重要的。 您不希望您的代码因为从笔记本电脑切换到台式机而变得一团糟。 因此，请尝试对您在项目中使用的工具、包和依赖项进行一些排序，并开发一种方法来为您用于项目的每台计算机执行此操作。 这就像拥有一双您每天都穿的最喜欢的运动鞋：舒适可靠。 使用 npm 或 pip 等包管理器来管理依赖项，因为这样您就可以非常轻松地在每台计算机上安装所有必需的包。 相信我，它会让您免于一一手动安装所有东西的麻烦。 没有人有时间这样做。

开发工具

在所有计算机上使用相同的工具和软件至关重要。 通过确保每个人都拥有相同的工具，您将最大限度地减少兼容性问题并创建统一的开发体验。 这就像举办一场编程派对，每个人都说同一种语言，跳着同一种节奏跳舞。

代码风格和格式

想象一下：您正在阅读一本书，字体在一页与一页之间随机变化。 令人沮丧，对吧？ 代码也是如此。 在您的团队中定义和实施一致的代码风格和格式指南非常重要。 使用 ESLint 或 Prettier 等 linter 和格式化程序自动格式化您的代码。 这就像拥有一根魔杖，可以为您清洁一切。 另外，它提高了可读性并减少了代码不一致，这对我来说是双赢的！

配置文件

在切换计算机时，这些可能会导致相当头疼。 您不希望每次更换计算机时都在寻找 API 密钥或调整数据库配置。 那只是浪费时间和精力。 所以这里是解决方案：创建一个专用存储库或共享文件夹来存储所有配置文件。 这包括特定于环境的设置、数据库配置、API 密钥以及项目所需的任何其他内容。 通过集中保存这些文件，您可以保持一致性并简化计算机之间的切换过程。

通过遵循这些最佳实践，计算机之间的切换压力会更小。 请记住，一致性是关键。 为您的开发环境带来一些秩序、建立代码风格指南和管理您的配置文件是这里的一些重要的关键要点。 所以，去吧，我的编码同志们，去征服世界吧。 一次一台电脑切换！

感谢您阅读 :)

一旦暂停，nielsington 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

原文:

In today's work environment, where remote work and flexible schedules are common, us developers often find ourselves switching between multiple computers. Sometimes it can get pretty messy, like trying to untangle a bunch of charging cables in your backpack. But fear not, because in this article we'll explore some strategies and best practices to make this whole computer-switching thing as smooth as butter on a hot pancake.

Consistency in your development environments

Switching computers can be a hassle, but if you would like to make the transition easier, it's crucial to establish some consistency in your development environments. You don't want your code to turn into a mess just because you switched from your laptop to your desktop. So, try to bring some order in the tools, packages, and dependencies you use in your project, and develop a way you'll do this for every computer you use for your project. It's like having a favorite pair of sneakers that you wear every day: comfortable and reliable. Use package managers like npm or pip to manage dependencies because this way you'll make it very easy for yourself to install all the required packages on each computer. Trust me, it'll save you from the headache of manually installing everything one by one. Nobody's got time for that.

Development tools

It's crucial to use the same tools and software across all computers. By making sure everyone has the same tools, you'll minimize compatibility issues and create a unified development experience. It's like having a coding party where everyone speaks the same language and dances to the same beat.

Code style and formatting

Picture this: you're reading a book, and the font keeps changing randomly from page to page. Frustrating, right? The same goes for code. It's important to define and enforce consistent code style and formatting guidelines within your team. Use linters and formatters like ESLint or Prettier to automatically format your code. It's like having a magic wand which makes everything clean for you. Plus, it improves readability and reduces code inconsistencies which sounds like a win-win to me!

Configuration files

These can cause quite a headache when switching computers. You don't want to be looking for API keys or tweaking database configurations every time you change computers. That's just wasted time and energy. So here's the solution: create a dedicated repository or shared folder to store all your configuration files. This includes environment-specific settings, database configurations, API keys, and anything else you need for your project. By keeping these files centralized, you maintain consistency and simplify the process of switching between computers.

By following these best practices, switching between computers will be less stressful. Remember, consistency is key. Bringing some order in your development environments, establishing code style guidelines and managing your configuration files are some of the important key takeaways here. So go forth, my coding comrades, and conquer the world. One computer switch at a time!

Thank you for reading :)

item.title: 将托管数据磁盘附加到 Azure WINDOWS 虚拟机 | ATTACHING A MANAGED DATA DISK TO AN AZURE WINDOWS VIRTUAL MACHINE
item.description:
伯纳德 Chika Uwaezuoke

发表于 6 月 17 日

本文介绍使用 Azure 门户将托管数据磁盘成功附加到 Windows 虚拟机所需的步骤。

为实现这一目标，需要完成两项主要任务。
什么是数据光盘，为什么它在计算中很重要？ 数据磁盘是附加到虚拟机的托管磁盘，用于存储应用程序数据或您需要保留的其他数据。

可用的数据磁盘类型是超级磁盘、高级固态驱动器 (SSD) 、标准 SSD和标准硬盘驱动器 (HDD) 。

托管磁盘具有许多优点，例如高耐用性和可用性、简单且可扩展、与可用性集集成、与可用区集成、享受Azure 备份支持和Azure 磁盘备份。

第 1 步：创建并附加数据磁盘

  • 登录到Azure 门户。

  • 搜索并选择虚拟机。

  • 从列表中选择一个虚拟机。

  • 在虚拟机窗格中，选择磁盘。

  • 在“磁盘”窗格中，选择“创建并附加新磁盘” 。

  • 在新磁盘的下拉列表中，进行所需的选择，然后为磁盘命名。

  • 选择“保存”以创建新数据磁盘并将其附加到 VM。

磁盘已创建并附加到 VM！

第二步：初始化新数据盘

  • 连接到虚拟机。 转到 VM 概览并单击连接。 然后单击“下载 RDP 文件”以将连接详细信息下载到您的桌面。

  • 转到下载并双击要运行的文件。 将弹出远程桌面连接提示。 单击连接。

输入虚拟机的用户名和密码。


这会将您远程连接到您的虚拟机。

在虚拟机中！

在正在运行的 VM 中选择 Windows 开始菜单，然后在搜索框中输入 diskmgmt.msc。 磁盘管理控制台打开。

磁盘管理识别出您有一个新的、未初始化的磁盘，并出现“初始化磁盘”窗口。

  • 确认已选择新磁盘，然后选择“确定”对其进行初始化。

新磁盘显示为未分配。

  • 右键单击磁盘上的任意位置并选择新建简单卷。 新建简单卷向导窗口打开。

  • 继续执行向导，保留所有默认设置，完成后选择完成。

将出现一个弹出窗口，通知您需要先格式化新磁盘才能使用它。 选择格式化磁盘。

在“格式化新磁盘”窗口中，检查设置，然后选择“开始”。
将出现一条警告，通知您格式化磁盘会擦除所有数据。 选择确定。

格式化完成后，选择确定。

数据盘健康，可以使用

虚拟桌面上的数据盘

希望这足够简单。

原文:

Bernard Chika Uwaezuoke

Posted on Jun 17

This article presents steps that are required to successfully attach a managed data disk to a Windows Virtual Machine using the Azure portal.

To achieve this, two major tasks need to be accomplished.
1. Create and attach a data disk
2. Initialize the new data disk

What is a data disc, and why is it important in computing? A data disk is a managed disk that's attached to a virtual machine to store application data, or other data you need to keep.

Azure managed disks are block-level storage volumes that are managed by Azure and used with Azure Virtual Machines.

The available types of data disks are ultra disks, premium solid-state drives (SSD), standard SSDs, and standard hard disk drives (HDD).

Managed disks come with many benefits such as high durability and availability, Simple and scalable, integration with availability sets, integration with Availability Zones, enjoys Azure Backup support and Azure Disk Backup.

STEP 1: CREATE AND ATTACH A DATA DISK

  • Sign in to the Azure portal.

  • Search for and select Virtual machines.

  • Select a virtual machine from the list.

  • On the Virtual machine pane, select Disks.

  • On the Disks pane, select Create and attach a new disk.

  • In the drop-downs for the new disk, make the selections you want, and name the disk.

  • Select Save to create and attach the new data disk to the VM.

The disk is created and attached to the VM!

STEP 2: INITIALIZE THE NEW DATA DISK

  • Connect to the VM. Go to the VM overview and click on connect. Then click on Download RDP File to download connecting details to your desktop.

  • Go to downloads and double-click on the file to run. The remote desktop connection prompt will pop-up. Click connect.

Windows security will pop-up, requesting for your credentials.

  • Enter the Username and Password of your virtual machine.


This will remotely connect you to your virtual machine.

In the Virtual Machine!

Select the Windows Start menu inside the running VM and enter diskmgmt.msc in the search box. The Disk Management console opens.

Disk Management recognizes that you have a new, uninitialized disk and the Initialize Disk window appears.

  • Verify that the new disk is selected and then select OK to initialize it.

The new disk appears as unallocated.

  • Right-click anywhere on the disk and select New simple volume. The New Simple Volume Wizard window opens.

  • Proceed through the wizard, keeping all of the defaults, and when you're done select Finish.

A pop-up window appears notifying you that you need to format the new disk before you can use it. Select Format disk.

In the Format new disk window, check the settings, and then select Start.
A warning appears notifying you that formatting the disks erases all of the data. Select OK.

When the formatting is complete, select OK.

Data disk healthy and ready for use

The data disk on the virtual desktop

The data disk on the VM in Azure portal

Hope this is simple enough.

Sardar Mudassar Ali Khan - Jun 16

Sardar Mudassar Ali Khan - Jun 16
